channel.title: Outpatient Services on Tokyo Police Hospital
channel.description: Recent content in Outpatient Services on Tokyo Police Hospital

item.title: First Visit (New Patients)
item.description:
Before Your First Visit

If you are visiting Tokyo Police Hospital for the first time, please review the information below carefully before your appointment.

Opening Hours

Time
Main Entrance Opens 7:45
Reception Hours (Morning) 8:00 – 11:30
Reception Hours (Afternoon) 12:30 – 16:00

Required Documents

Please bring the following items to the reception desk on your first visit:

  1. Health Insurance Card (or MyNumber Card / Individual Number Card for online insurance verification)
  2. Referral Letter from your regular physician or clinic (strongly recommended)
  3. Medical history summary (if available)
  4. List of current medications (prescription notebooks, medication lists, or actual medications)
  5. Any relevant previous test results (X-rays, blood test results, etc.)
  6. Emergency contact information

Initial Consultation Surcharge

In accordance with national healthcare guidelines, patients who visit without a referral letter from another medical institution are required to pay an initial consultation surcharge of ¥7,700 (tax included) in addition to normal medical fees.

item.title: Repeat Visit (Returning Patients)
item.description:
Repeat Visit Information

For patients who have previously been seen at Tokyo Police Hospital, please follow the procedures below when scheduling and attending your follow-up visits.

Booking a Repeat Appointment

Repeat Appointment Center
Phone 03-5343-5620 / 03-5343-5621
Available Hours Monday – Saturday, 8:15 – 17:00

You can schedule, change, or cancel appointments by telephone. Please have your patient ID number ready when calling.

Online Appointment Booking

Repeat appointments may also be made online via the hospital’s patient portal. Please ask reception for details.

item.title: Referral Letter
item.description:
Referral Letters (Shōkaijō)

A referral letter (shōkaijō, 紹介状) is a document issued by your regular physician, clinic, or another hospital that introduces you to a specialist at Tokyo Police Hospital. We strongly encourage all new patients to obtain a referral letter before visiting.

Benefits of Bringing a Referral Letter

  • No initial consultation surcharge: Patients with a referral letter are exempt from the ¥7,700 surcharge.
  • Faster and more efficient care: The referring physician provides relevant medical history, test results, and diagnosis notes, allowing our specialists to assess your condition more quickly.
  • Continuity of care: After your treatment at our hospital, a report will be sent back to your referring physician, ensuring coordinated care.

How to Obtain a Referral Letter

  1. Visit your regular clinic or primary care physician.
  2. Explain that you wish to be referred to Tokyo Police Hospital and the specialty you need.
  3. Your doctor will prepare a referral letter for you.
  4. Bring the sealed referral letter to our reception on the day of your visit.

Medical Liaison Office

For referring physicians and healthcare institutions wishing to send patients to Tokyo Police Hospital:

item.title: Referral-Focused Medical Institution
item.description:
What Is a Referral-Focused Medical Institution?

Tokyo Police Hospital has been designated as a Referral-Focused Medical Institution (Shōkai Jushū Jūten Iryō Kikan, 紹介受診重点医療機関) under Japan’s national healthcare framework.

This designation recognizes hospitals that primarily provide advanced and specialized medical care for complex cases requiring hospital-level expertise, while encouraging patients with stable or routine conditions to consult with local clinics and general practitioners.

Why This Designation Matters

Japan’s healthcare system is structured so that:

item.title: Medication
item.description:
Medication at Tokyo Police Hospital

Our goal is to ensure that every patient receives safe, effective, and appropriate medication. Please read the following information carefully.

Hospital Pharmacy

The hospital pharmacy is located on the 1st Floor. After your consultation, your physician will issue an electronic prescription, which can be filled at:

  • Hospital Pharmacy (1F) – for immediate pickup on the same day
  • Any community pharmacy of your choice (outside prescription)

We recommend using a designated “Family Pharmacy” (Kari Yakkyoku) near your home for continuity of medication management.
